formulas.name: Fisher's Equation of Exchange
formulas.units: No units (variables are in terms of money and transactions)
formulas.notes: Used to analyze the relationship between money supply, velocity of money, price level, and volume of trade

formulas.name: Friedman's Formula

variables.k: Proportion of income held as cash

formulas.units: No units (variables are in terms of money and transactions)
formulas.notes: Used to analyze the demand for money in terms of income and price level

formulas.name: Quantity Theory of Money

variables.k: Proportion of income held as cash

formulas.units: No units (variables are in terms of money and transactions)
formulas.notes: States that the money supply is a function of the price level and national income

formulas.name: Fisher's Ideal Currency Standard
formulas.formula: Price of foreign currency = Exchange rate

variables.Exchange rate: Rate at which one currency is exchanged for another

formulas.units: No units (exchange rate is a ratio)
formulas.notes: Describes a currency standard in which the price of a foreign currency is fixed at a specific rate

formulas.name: Keynesian Cross (IS-LM Model)
formulas.units: No units (variables are in terms of money and transactions)
formulas.notes: Describes the relationship between investment, national income, taxes, money supply, and price level in the IS curve

formulas.name: IS-LM Model
formulas.units: No units (variables are in terms of money and transactions)
formulas.notes: Combines the IS and LM curves to analyze the short-run equilibrium of the economy

formulas.name: Monetary Policy Formula
formulas.notes: Describes the relationship between the interest rate, nominal interest rate, inflation rate, real interest rate, and expected inflation rate in monetary policy

formulas.name: Fisher Effect
formulas.notes: States that the nominal interest rate is equal to the real interest rate plus the expected inflation rate

formulas.name: Liquidity Preference Theory
formulas.units: No units (variables are in terms of money and transactions)
formulas.notes: Describes the liquidity preference function in terms of the money supply, price level, nominal interest rate, and inflation rate
